commit.message:
fix: ClassifyByKeywords recognises CI/CD patterns (ci/ prefix) (#147)

'CI/CD pipeline completed' was not classified as build because
ClassifyByKeywords only checked for 'ci ' (trailing space), missing
the 'CI/CD' style where a slash follows 'CI'.

Adding the 'ci/' check catches:
  - 'CI/CD pipeline completed'
  - 'CI/CD workflow triggered'
  - Any pattern where 'CI' is immediately followed by a slash

False-positive analysis: common English words containing 'ci' (recipe,
social, special, facial...) are followed by letters, not '/', so the
new check introduces no false matches.

Regression tests: two new InlineData entries in KeywordFallback_BackwardCompatible.

commit.message:
perf: replace block_in_place+block_on with sync cache in consensus

Add parking_lot::RwLock-backed cached_active / cached_all snapshot fields
to MasternodeRegistry. The caches are rebuilt inside each masternodes write
lock scope (register_internal, deregister, unregister, cleanup_stale_reports,
apply_masternode_reg squatter eviction, apply_collateral_unlock), guaranteeing
the snapshots are always consistent with the underlying HashMap.

Replace all 7 block_in_place(|| block_on(registry.list_active/all())) calls
in consensus.rs (get_validators, accumulate_timevote, verify_timeproof,
check_prepare_consensus, check_precommit_consensus, get_masternodes,
get_masternodes_with_rewards) with direct active_masternodes_cached() /
all_masternodes_cached() calls.

Impact:
- Eliminates tokio OS-thread spawning under concurrent vote load (block_in_place
  converts a worker thread to a blocking thread; under 4+ concurrent callers
  tokio had to spawn extra OS threads, growing unboundedly during flood attacks)
- Consensus functions now run in ~100 ns (parking_lot read + Vec clone) instead
  of crossing the async/blocking boundary
- RPC and networking tasks no longer starve while consensus holds worker threads
- Fixes runtime starvation on 1-core / 2-thread VPS hosts where block_in_place
  could freeze the entire tokio runtime

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

When the underlying port-forward dies (pod restart, network timeout),
the proxy now detects the connection failure and transparently
re-establishes the port-forward to a new controller pod. Requests
that trigger the reconnect are retried once after the new connection
is established.

The proxyBackend struct implements http.RoundTripper with:
- Connection health check via TCP dial before each request
- Automatic reconnect on connection refused or dial errors
- Pod re-discovery on reconnect (picks up new pods after restarts)
- Thread-safe backend port and transport management

commit.message:
fix: prevent pull-to-refresh transform from breaking fixed-position modals

The PullToRefresh component always applied transform: translateY(0px) and
will-change: transform to .ptr-content, even when idle. A CSS transform on
any ancestor creates a new containing block for position: fixed descendants,
causing modals (Edit Device, Share, etc.) to be positioned relative to the
wrapper instead of the viewport.

Only apply transform and will-change when actively pulling (pullDistance > 0).

commit.message:
r600: upgrade KCACHE from UNPROVEN to UNSAFE with ISA evidence

ISA §9.2 confirms KCACHE has NO buffer SIZE field — only 256-byte
cache-line boundaries.  Relative-indexed OOB returns NaN (0x7FFFFFFF),
static OOB returns undefined, within-line-past-size leaks cache contents.
None satisfy Vulkan robustness (zero return required).

Cross-document update (all 3 sources now aligned):
- ABI contract §5: KCACHE declared UNSAFE, ISA evidence cited
- ABI contract §10 Gap 1: upgraded from UNPROVEN to CONFIRMED UNSAFE
- ABI contract §10 Gap 3: added per-thread masking ISA confirmation
- ABI contract §11 Q1: probe now confirmatory, not blocking
- Blueprint robust_read_access: ISA §9.2 NaN behavior documented
- OOB findings table: KCACHE row changed from ⚠️ UNPROVEN to ❌ UNSAFE

commit.message:
r600: Phase 6 probe designs v2 — rubber-duck review fixes

Major changes from rubber-duck critique:
- Merge Probes 1+4 into single KCACHE probe with static and relative sub-tests
- Fix VTX/TEX routing confusion: rename Probe 2 to TEX (samplerBuffer uses TEX,
  not VTX), downgrade priority to MEDIUM
- Drop Probes 5 (VTX byte boundary, same TEX routing issue) and original Probe 3
  (COMP_MASK, cannot prove through GLSL)
- Strengthen Probe 3 (thread isolation) with nonzero 0xAAAAAAAA canary to catch
  spurious zero-writes on masked lanes
- Add Global Methodology Requirements section: ISA capture mandatory for all
  probes, integer bit-preserving output, nonzero canaries, GLSL-level in-bounds
- KCACHE probe now uses 1024-byte backing buffer with 3 distinct canary zones
  to distinguish cache-line leak vs NaN vs zero-fill
- Add ISA verification criteria for each probe (confirm hardware unit routing)

Revised priority: KCACHE (CRITICAL) > RAT thread mask (HIGH) > TEX exact
byte (MEDIUM, reframed) > RAT ARRAY_SIZE (MEDIUM, confirmatory)

commit.message:
r600: Phase 6 hardware probe designs from ISA deep-dive

Six probes designed from 6 independent ISA research passes across
Evergreen and R600 manuals. Key ISA findings driving the designs:

- KCACHE OOB returns NaN (0x7FFFFFFF), not zero — unsafe for robustness
- MEM_RAT uses per-thread active masking (ISA §10.2.2)
- VTX SIZE_MINUS_ONE has no documented alignment requirement
- IF/ENDIF costs 3 CF slots + 1 packable ALU cycle

Probes ordered by priority:
1. KCACHE OOB (CRITICAL) — UBO robustness path selection
2. VTX exact-byte SIZE (HIGH) — eliminate ALIGN_POT tail violation
3. MEM_RAT thread isolation (HIGH) — confirm per-thread write masking
4-6. Refinement probes (MEDIUM)

Includes ISA-grounded rebuttals of reviewer's 3 gap claims.

fix: clear stale running flags on startup; fix Stop All with orphaned DB state

After a server restart, the in-memory _bots dict is empty but the DB
may still have running=1 entries from the previous process. This caused
the frontend to enable the Stop button (reading DB state), but clicking
Stop returned 'No bots are running' (checking the empty _bots dict).

Fixes:
- db.reset_running_flags(): resets running=0 for all symbols in DB
- app.py: calls reset_running_flags() at module load so every fresh
  server start begins with a clean slate
- api_stop(): also clears orphaned DB running=1 flags for symbols whose
  in-memory bot is gone, so the UI stays consistent

Co-authored-by: magyargergo <11230420+magyargergo@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf(SM-9): address PR #740 review comments

- Eliminate double direct lookup in resolveMethodByOwner: delegate
  straight to lookupMethodByOwnerWithMRO when a HeritageMap is
  available (the MRO helper already does the direct lookup before
  walking ancestors). Fallback path handles the no-HeritageMap case.
- Memoize C3 linearization per HeritageMap via a WeakMap keyed cache.
  HeritageMap is immutable after build, so C3 results are stable for
  its lifetime; WeakMap lets the cache auto-drain when the HeritageMap
  is GC'd. Null sentinel caches linearization failures so cyclic
  hierarchies are not reprocessed. Eliminates per-call buildParentMap +
  c3Linearize on Python codebases.
- ancestors variable typed as readonly to accept the cached result
  without copying.
- Add four missing MRO unit tests: Kotlin implements-split, C#
  implements-split, JavaScript first-wins (separate provider from TS),
  and C++ leftmost-base diamond (first diamond test for C++).

* fix(SM-9): CI prettier + address PR #740 follow-up review

- Fix CI prettier failure in test/integration/resolvers/java.test.ts
  (auto-formatted — was introduced in 37563a31 before my first fix
  commit but had not been caught locally).
- Pin caller on the SM-9 Java integration test (parentMethodCall.source
  === 'run') so a regression that misattributes the CALLS edge fails.
- Add two implements-split unit tests:
  * Ambiguous default from two interfaces → BFS first-wins. Pins the
    contract that lookupMethodByOwnerWithMRO returns a defined result
    (full ambiguity detection is deferred to computeMRO graph pass).
  * Class method precedence over interface default: Child extends Base
    implements IFoo where both define handle() — documents that BFS
    visits the extends edge first, matching Java's class-wins rule.
- Add @internal JSDoc on lookupMethodByOwnerWithMRO clarifying it is
  exported only for testing; resolveMethodByOwner is the proper entry
  point for callers.

* test(SM-9): per-language integration fixtures and tests for inherited method resolution

Extends the SM-9 integration coverage beyond Java with six new
child-extends-parent fixtures, one per MRO strategy:

- python-child-extends-parent       → C3 strategy
- typescript-child-extends-parent   → first-wins
- javascript-child-extends-parent   → first-wins (separate provider)
- kotlin-child-extends-parent       → implements-split
- csharp-child-extends-parent       → implements-split
- cpp-child-extends-parent          → leftmost-base

Each fixture follows the java-child-extends-parent pattern:
- Parent class with a single method
- Child class extending Parent, no override
- App class/function that instantiates Child and calls
  the parent method — exercises the full ingestion pipeline,
  HeritageMap construction, and lookupMethodByOwnerWithMRO walk.

For every fixture the matching integration test asserts:
- Parent and Child classes are detected
- Child → Parent EXTENDS edge is emitted
- The parent-method call resolves to the correct target file
- The caller is pinned (source === 'run' / 'Run') to catch
  edge misattribution regressions

Rust is intentionally omitted — its qualified-syntax strategy
returns undefined from lookupMethodByOwnerWithMRO by design, so
there is no inherited-method resolution to assert against.

All 1739 integration resolver tests pass (+18 new SM-9 tests
across 6 languages).

fix: persist CSRF cookie and stop caching /api/session

Two root causes for session loss after PWA reopen / redeploy:

1. gorilla/csrf cookie was a session cookie (no MaxAge). Mobile OSes
   clear session cookies when killing the PWA process, causing CSRF
   validation failures on reopen. Fix: set MaxAge to 1 year and
   SameSite=Lax to match the session cookie behavior.

2. Service worker cached GET /api/session responses with a 15-minute
   TTL. Cached responses lose Set-Cookie headers and carry stale
   X-CSRF-Token values, breaking the double-submit CSRF pattern.
   Fix: remove /api/session from cache config entirely.

Bump service worker cache version to v4 to clear stale entries.

Also fixes E2E test selectors (ptr-container, simplified mileage test).

commit.message:
fix: clip terminal content at right edge to prevent column overflow (#100)

xterm.js v6 intentionally leaves overflow:hidden commented out on its
ScrollableElement wrapper (to allow decorations to escape). Combined with
FitAddon subtracting a hardcoded 14px DEFAULT_SCROLL_BAR_WIDTH that may
not match the native Windows scrollbar width, the terminal can render ~1
column wider than the visible area — producing orphaned characters at the
right edge.

Fix:
- Add overflow:hidden on .terminal-surface and .xterm to clip stray pixels
- Hide the native scrollbar on .xterm-viewport (the custom
  SmoothScrollableElement scrollbar handles all interaction)
- Apply same fix to analysis-window__terminal for consistency

Co-authored-by: heping duan <hduan10@ford.com>
